Nice, interesting, we see that, we see that a lot too, so we can put a pin in this one for now, so if we came in and we interviewed your three best customers, doesn't have to be the biggest, but the best, what are they saying Relay is uniquely good at, the biggest value props and drivers that they have?

I think one of the things we're most well known for is that, and I'm probably not going to say it in the most technical way, we should have asked a few people before we came, so we can get you a better way to say this, but one of the things we're best known for is that you can split your revenue into various checking accounts.
A lot of bigger banks force you to pay a fee on every checking account you open, and that's really difficult for small business owners.
It's because they kind of use their accounts as holding spaces for money.
So they may want, let's say they're a real estate investor, they may want each of their homes to have a different bank account.
So they can end up paying thousands of dollars of fees a month to have multiple bank accounts.
Relay, excuse me, Relay has the option, I think it's up to 20, between 20 and 50 accounts based on your plan that you can have open for your business without fees.
Or with like ultra, ultra low fee.
I think it's no fees at this point.
So a lot of folks end up using us for that.
And there's a method of small business finance called Profit First that we're known for working really well with.
We actually are partnered up with the founder of that business and that methodology.
It wasn't on purpose.
We're just like so fit to purpose for his methodology that it's become this really beautiful partnership.
And a lot of people that subscribe.
His style of small business money management end up becoming Relay customers.
That's cool.
Yeah.
So that's a really big one.
And it's good, too, because I think an important thing to know about our customer is that they tend to be trapped in the present.
So if they're very successful, even if they're, I should say, even if they're very successful, they're making a million plus dollars a month or a year in revenue.
They often log into their business accounts, see that they have money, and spend that money.
So it's very present-focused.

And what we're trying and what Profit First tries to get them to do is to say, the money came in, but you need to set a little bit aside for profit, a little bit aside for taxes, a little bit aside for people spend, a little bit aside for operating.
Like, it helps you kind of divide it out and not overspend.
So that's a really big one.

You can set up recurring invoices.

Like it's just exactly, precisely set up for how a small business owner needs to work.
